page: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a virtual server belonging to the High Performance family, configured with 12 vCPUs and 24.0 GB of system memory. The processing power is delivered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This configuration features 12 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model and a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. Local storage is bundled with a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations place the server's compute capabilities in the average tier, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2219.48 and a multi-core score of 27275.02. Passmark CPU testing yields a score of 17153.85, while database operations score 3550.56. Memory performance presents some tradeoffs; while cached small-block read bandwidth reaches 1019.76 GB/sec, the Passmark memory score is weak at 1623.4, and large-block uncached write bandwidth is measured at 44.07 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks show performance limitations, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs in the average range at 41.52 GB/sec.

Given the shared CPU allocation and a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is capable of handling static web serving, which benchmarked at 7.88 GB/sec, and Redis operations, which achieved 11.99 million operations per second. The hardware supports light AI workloads, processing small 135M LLM prompts at 521.62 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak 16.57 tokens per second for medium 7B LLM prompt processing. This server is suitable for multi-threaded file processing, web serving, and database operations.
description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Performance family, featuring 12 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 24.0 GB of RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core compute capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 27275.02 and a Passmark CPU score of 17153.85. However, memory performance is weak, as reflected in a Passmark memory score of 1623.4, and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the bottom 10% tier. The server demonstrates average capabilities in static web serving at 7.88 GB/sec and Redis operations at 11.99 million operations per second. This instance represents a resource-dense option for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, and database operations that can tolerate shared CPU allocation and lower single-threaded performance.
og_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 12 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 24.0 GB RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering average multi-core performance with some single-threaded memory tradeoffs.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd features 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe storage, offering balanced multi-core performance.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual server with twelve shared AMD EPYC cores and NVMe storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 12 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz
24.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 27275.02
Tradeoffs include weak single-threaded compression and memory scores
Suitable for static web serving, Redis databases, and multi-threaded processing
